name: minimal-snowplow-tracker

metadata.name: minimal-snowplow-tracker
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: A minimal snowplow event tracker for Python. Add analytics to your Python and Django apps, webapps and games

documentation:
# Python Analytics for Snowplow

```{image} https://circleci.com/gh/fishtown-analytics/snowplow-python-tracker/tree/master.svg?style=svg
:alt: Build Status
:target: https://circleci.com/gh/fishtown-analytics/snowplow-python-tracker
```

## Overview

Add analytics to your Python apps and Python games with the [Snowplow] event tracker for [Python].

With this tracker you can collect event data from your Python-based applications, games or Python web servers/frameworks.

This is a fork of the original Snowplow Python tracker v0.8.0, with a number of changes:
: - All emitters except for the basic one removed
  - Tests converted to docker
  - All tracking event methods except for structured/unstructured removed
  - Python 3.7 supported

## Contributing quickstart

Assuming Git and [Docker] (with docker-compose) are installed:

```
host$ git clone git@github.com:snowplow/snowplow-python-tracker.git
host$ docker-compose run --rm test tox
```

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.tracker.Tracker.__init__
documentation:
---
param app_id: Application ID
param emitters: Emitters to which events will be sent
param encode_base64: Whether JSONs in the payload should be base-64 encoded
param namespace: Identifier for the Tracker instance
param subject: Subject to be tracked
type  app_id: string_or_none
type  emitters: list\[>0\](emitter) | emitter
type  encode_base64: bool
type  namespace: string_or_none
type  subject: subject | None
---

documentation:
---
param tstamp: User-input timestamp or None
rtype: int
type  tstamp: int | float | None
---

documentation:
Send the payload to a emitter

:param  pb:              Payload builder
:type   pb:              payload

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.tracker.Tracker.complete_payload
documentation:
Called by all tracking events to add the standard name-value pairs
to the Payload object irrespective of the tracked event.

:param  pb:              Payload builder
:type   pb:              payload
:param  context:         Custom context for the event
:type   context:         context_array | None

:type   tstamp:          timestamp | int | float | None
:rtype:                  tracker

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.tracker.Tracker.track_struct_event
documentation:
:param  category:       Category of the event
:type   category:       non_empty_string
:param  action:         The event itself
:type   action:         non_empty_string
:param  label:          Refer to the object the action is
                        performed on
:type   label:          string_or_none
:param  property_:      Property associated with either the action
                        or the object
:type   property_:      string_or_none
:param  value:          A value associated with the user action
:type   value:          int | float | None
:param  context:        Custom context for the event
:type   context:        context_array | None
:rtype:                 tracker

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.tracker.Tracker.track_unstruct_event
documentation:
:param  event_json:      The properties of the event. Has two field:
                         A "data" field containing the event properties and
                         A "schema" field identifying the schema against which the data is validated
:type   event_json:      self_describing_json
:param  context:         Custom context for the event
:type   context:         context_array | None
:param  tstamp:          User-set timestamp
:type   tstamp:          timestamp | int | None
:rtype:                  tracker

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.tracker.Tracker.flush
documentation:
Flush the emitter

:param  asynchronous:  Whether the flush is done asynchronously. Default is False
:type   asynchronous:  bool
:rtype:         tracker

classes.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.__init__
documentation:
---
param buffer_size: The maximum number of queued events before the buffer is flushed.
  Default is 10.
param byte_limit: The size event list after reaching which queued events will be flushed
param endpoint: The collector URL. Don't include "<http://>" - this is done automatically.
param method: The HTTP request method
param on_failure: |-
  Callback executed if at least one HTTP request in a flush has status code 200
  Gets passed two arguments:
  1\) The number of events which were successfully sent
  2\) If method is "post": The unsent data in string form;

  > If method is "get":  An array of dictionaries corresponding to the unsent events' payloads
param on_success: |-
  Callback executed after every HTTP request in a flush has status code 200
  Gets passed the number of events flushed.
param port: The collector port to connect to
param protocol: The protocol to use - http or https. Defaults to http.
type  buffer_size: int | None
type  byte_limit: int | None
type  endpoint: string
type  method: method
type  on_failure: function | None
type  on_success: function | None
type  port: int | None
type  protocol: protocol
---

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.as_collector_uri
documentation:
---
param endpoint: The raw endpoint provided by the user
param method: Either `get` or `post` HTTP method
param port: The collector port to connect to
param protocol: The protocol to use - http or https
rtype: string
type  endpoint: string
type  method: method
type  port: int | None
type  protocol: protocol
---

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.input
documentation:
Adds an event to the buffer.
If the maximum size has been reached, flushes the buffer.

```
param payload

The name-value pairs for the event

type  payload

dict(string:*)

source:5: (WARNING/2) Inline emphasis start-string without end-string.
```

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.reached_limit
documentation:
Checks if event-size or bytes limit are reached

```
rtype

bool
```

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.flush
documentation: Sends all events in the buffer to the collector.

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.http_post
documentation:
---
param data: The array of JSONs to be sent
type  data: string
---

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.http_get
documentation:
---
param payload: The event properties
type  payload: dict(string:)
---

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.sync_flush
documentation:
Calls the flush method of the base Emitter class.
This is guaranteed to be blocking, not asynchronous.

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.is_good_status_code
documentation:
---
param status_code: HTTP status code
rtype: bool
type  status_code: int
---

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.send_events
documentation:
---
param evts: Array of events to be sent
type  evts: list(dict(string:))
---

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.set_flush_timer
documentation:
Set an interval at which the buffer will be flushed

```
param timeout

interval in seconds

type  timeout

int | float

param flush_now

immediately flush buffer

type  flush_now

bool
```

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.cancel_flush_timer
documentation: Abort automatic async flushing

methods.name: snowplow_tracker.emitters.Emitter.attach_sent_timestamp
documentation:
Attach (by mutating in-place) current timestamp in milliseconds
as `stm` param

```
param events

Array of events to be sent

type  events

list(dict(string:*))

source:5: (WARNING/2) Inline emphasis start-string without end-string.

rtype

None
```

Synchronously send Snowplow events to a Snowplow collector
Supports both GET and POST requests
